channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Los libros de no ficción más esperados del verano
item.description: Memorias, historias, crímenes reales, investigaciones y mucho más.

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Nicolas Cage habla sobre convertirse en un meme
item.description: El icónico actor se convirtió en un meme, en un mito y en alguien extraordinario.
item.dc:creator: David Marchese

media:content.media:credit: Devin Oktar Yalkin for The New York Times

item.title: Hungría demostró cómo derrotar a un autócrata
item.description: Vencer a Viktor Orbán tras 16 años en el poder parecía una misión imposible; su derrota encierra lecciones claras para Estados Unidos.
item.dc:creator: M. Gessen and Máté Bartha

media:content.media:credit: Máté Bartha for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Backrooms’ y el auge de las películas de creadores de Youtube
item.description: Durante años, Hollywood se preguntó si el éxito de plataformas como YouTube podría trasladarse a la pantalla grande. La respuesta está resonando en las taquillas.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

media:content.media:credit: Asterios Moutsokapas/A24, vía Associated Press
media:content.media:description: El director de Backrooms, Kane Parsons, izquierda, en el set con una de las estrellas de la nueva película, Chiwetel Ejiofor.

item.title: Israel Day Parade to Return to New York Against a Changed Backdrop
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani does not plan to attend the annual Israel Day Parade, which is taking place amid a deterioration of public support for the country and rising antisemitism across the United States.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Police Commissioner Jessica Tisch described the parade as “one of the most joyful days of the year.” Mayor Zohran Mamdani said security would be tighter than ever.

item.title: ‘The Ingredients Looked Good, So I Told Him He Should Go for It’
item.description: Help with a hamburger, getting a look under a stranger’s hood and more reader tales of New York City in this week’s Metropolitan Diary.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 31, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: High-Speed Sailboats Race Past the New York Skyline
item.description: SailGP, a two-day competition some say is like Formula 1 on the water, has replaced part of the normal traffic in New York Harbor with high-tech catamarans.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein

media:content.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Coast Guard cordoned off a race area New York Harbor, which is usually crammed with vessels.

item.title: Where Is Toronto’s Best Party? Try the Basement.
item.description: Millennials who yearn for a bygone era of dancing that feels pure and more organic are bringing back the basement jam, popularized decades ago by Toronto’s Caribbean diaspora.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai and Ian Willms

media:content.media:description: Unlike most nightclubs, the Caribbean-style basement jams don’t adhere to strict dress-codes, and there are no private booths. The parties hark back to a simpler time.

item.title: Looking Back on a Year as Co-Chief Restaurant Critics
item.description: Tejal Rao and Ligaya Mishan, who were appointed last June, discuss some of their culinary highlights, including barbecue in Texas and Bangladeshi food in New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

media:content.media:credit: Yuvraj Khanna for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ligaya Mishan said she loved writing about Korai Kitchen in Jersey City, where “they just serve you food for hours.”

item.title: 5 riesgos de consumir demasiada proteína
item.description: Desde enfermedades cardíacas hasta problemas renales, los expertos explican los riesgos de consumir mucho más proteína de la que el cuerpo necesita.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Egan

item.title: Men and Women Should Be Optimists About Love, Not Heteropessimists
item.description: “Heteropessimism” is all the rage — but really, there’s never been a better time to be looking for love. Go on, be a hetero-optimist.
item.dc:creator: Magdalene J. Taylor

item.title: In Taiwan, ‘Mainland Spouses’ From China Become a Focus of Infiltration Fears
item.description: A Chinese-born woman accused of infiltration has set off a debate over how Taiwan defends its democracy without profiling migrants from China.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley and Amy Chang Chien

media:content.media:credit: Lam Yik Fei for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A village near Taipei. Taiwan officials are confronting what they say is growing Chinese infiltration of the island’s politics, media and internet.

item.title: 11 Dead as All Bodies Recovered After Washington Chemical Explosion
item.description: The remains of the two workers who were still missing after the blast this week at a Washington State paper mill were recovered on Saturday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

media:content.media:credit: David Ryder/Reuters
media:content.media:description: A drone view of a chemical tank after its rupture at the Nippon Dynawave Packaging pulp and paper mill in Longview, Washington this week.

item.title: What Trump Actually Wants From the G.O.P.
item.description: “You can be the kingmaker even when you’re not the king,” argues the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein. The Republican strategist Liam Donovan joins “The Ezra Klein Show” to explain that Trump’s core strategy is ensuring a vulnerable G.O.P. can never abandon him.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Book Review: ‘Girl’s Girl,’ by Sonia Feldman
item.description: In Sonia Feldman’s novel, “Girl’s Girl,” the delicate balance of a Gen Z friend group is unsettled over one Ohio summer.
item.dc:creator: Sanjena Sathian

item.title: An Afternoon in Brooklyn with Mayor Mamdani and a Bar Full of Arsenal Fans
item.description: Zohran Mamdani watched his favorite team, Arsenal, compete for the Champions League title with fans at a bar, getting a rare taste of his old life after a meteoric rise in politics.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy

media:content.media:credit: Adrienne Grunwald for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani and Spike Lee, two of the best known Arsenal fans in the United States, had reason to be hopeful during the early stages of the match.

item.title: Trump Sends Tougher Terms to Iran for Peace Framework, Officials Say
item.description: His changes to the proposed deal were potentially designed to speed up the process by putting pressure Iran to accept the current framework, one official said.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Ronen Bergman and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A woman holds a photo of Mojtaba Khamenei, the supreme leader, at a recent memorial ceremony in Tehran.

item.title: Inside Trump’s Deal With the I.R.S. to Drop His $10 Billion Lawsuit
item.description: Discussions among a group of lawyers with allegiance to the president were closely held. Some senior White House officials were said to have felt blindsided as the agreement took shape.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer, Andrew Duehren, Glenn Thrush, Ben Protess and Maggie Haberman

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times

item.title: After N.J. Governor’s Call for Calm, Protesters Clash With Police at Delaney Hall
item.description: Protesters clashed with the New Jersey State Police and Newark police officers outside the immigration detention center Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Maia Coleman and Mark Bonamo

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: There have been demonstrations outside the immigration detention center, Delaney Hall, for a week in protest of living conditions in the facility.

item.title: Edgar Morin, ‘Grandfather’ of French Intellectuals, Dies at 104
item.description: A former member of the Resistance, he went on to a career spanning eras and disciplines. His books and pronouncements carried moral authority.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter

media:content.media:credit: Martin Bureau/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Edgar Morin in 2003. “I have a permanent sense of the mystery of all things,” he said. “I still have no idea why I was born, why I exist.”

item.title: C.I.A. Officer Arrested With Gold Once Worked With No. 2 Pentagon Official
item.description: David Rush, a longtime C.I.A. officer, appears to have first had contact with Stephen A. Feinberg during President Trump’s first term. Some officials said the two men were not close.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Mark Mazzetti
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The C.I.A. officer arrested last week with more than $40 million in gold bars in his home had previously worked with Stephen A. Feinberg, now the deputy secretary of defense.

item.title: W.H.O. Chief Visits Ebola-Struck Region: ‘It’s Time to Move Fast’
item.description: Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the director general of the World Health Organization, told The New York Times on a flight to the Democratic Republic of Congo that swift international support was necessary to contain the Ebola virus, which is spreading rapidly there.
item.dc:creator: Bethlehem Feleke, Michael Anthony Adams and Alisa Shodiyev Kaff

media:content.media:credit: Michael Anthony Adams/The New York Times

item.title: Senator Dan Sullivan Has a New Challenger in Alaska: Dan Sullivan
item.description: Two candidates with the same name will be on the ballot seeking the same seat in the state’s primary. “It’s going to be confusing,” one Republican said.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Sullivan for Senate; Eric Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dan J. Sullivan, left, is challenging Senator Dan Sullivan, Republican of Alaska, for his seat.

item.title: Loud Booms Heard Along the East Coast This Week
item.description: Loud booms were heard in the Boston area on Saturday and in South Carolina on Thursday. NASA said the noise in Boston was caused by a fireball piercing the air; the cause of the South Carolina boom is still unknown.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park/Rueters
media:content.media:description: For the second time in a week, a loud boom has sounded on the East Coast. This time, on Saturday, it was in the Boston area.

item.title: Target Practice
item.description: Jeffrey Martinovic and Evan Park hit the bull’s-eye with their Sunday puzzle collaboration.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: Fabrice Coffrini/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Clear eyes and steady hands will get you through this solve.

item.title: Meteor Over Boston Area Causes Loud Boom That Puzzled Residents
item.description: A fireball reached 75,000 miles per hour before breaking up miles above New England, NASA said, causing a boom that alarmed residents. The incident followed a similar event in South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Mokam

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park/Rueters
media:content.media:description: For the second time in a week, a loud boom has sounded on the East Coast. This time, on Saturday, it was in the Boston area.

item.title: Trump Urges Canceling Freedom 250 Concerts After Artists Drop Out
item.description: The president had earlier said on social media that he should take the place of “these highly paid, Third Rate ‘Artists,’” saying he gets “larger audiences than Elvis.”
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump at an event in Suffern, N.Y., last week.

item.title: What to Know About the ICE Agent Arrested in Texas for a Shooting in Minnesota
item.description: The agent was charged in Minnesota with assault and filing a false police report in the shooting of an immigrant from Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Mokam

media:content.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Federal agents responded to protests in response to an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent’s shooting of a Venezuelan man in Minneapolis in January.

item.title: Una jueza reabre la demanda de Trump al IRS y cuestiona su fondo ‘antinstrumentalización’
item.description: La orden fue un golpe tanto para el presidente Trump, quien había desestimado voluntariamente la demanda la semana pasada, como para el Departamento de Justicia, que utilizó la demanda para establecer un fondo que podría estar destinado a los aliados de Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer and Andrew Duehren

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: En una severa orden el viernes, una jueza dijo que quería investigar las circunstancias que rodearon los esfuerzos del presidente Trump para resolver la demanda de una manera que lo beneficiara a él y a sus aliados.

item.title: Storm Blasts New England With Cold and Wind
item.description: Residents ready for summer lamented the unusual late May weather as tens of thousands of customers lost power in multiple states, including Connecticut and Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

item.title: Dang Van Phuoc Dies at 90; Intrepid Photojournalist in Vietnam War
item.description: A photographer for The Associated Press, he spent a decade on the front lines of combat in his native Vietnam and lost his right eye in a grenade explosion.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

item.title: Platner’s Wife Flagged His Sexual Texts With Other Women as Maine Senate Race Began
item.description: The wife of Graham Platner, the Democratic candidate in Maine, told the campaign he had sent sexual messages to other women.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck and Lisa Lerer

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, the Democratic candidate for Senate in Maine, during a campaign event in April.

item.title: Four More Miners Rescued From Flooded Cave in Laos
item.description: Four more miners were rescued from a flooded cave in Laos on Saturday after being trapped for more than a week. Five miners in total have been rescued, and crews are still searching for two more.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:credit: Association of Volunteers for Lao People, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Miners are tended by rescue worked after being freed on Saturday from a cave in central Laos.

item.title: Who Is Ahead in Washington's 5th Congressional District?
item.description: Track the latest polls in Washington's 5th Congressional District.

item.title: U.S. Military Kills 3 on Boat Suspected of Drug Trafficking
item.description: Around 200 people have now been killed in the attacks since last year, in an operation critics say has failed to slow the smuggling of cocaine.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gen. Francis L. Donovan, the head of the Southern Command, during a hearing in Washington in January.

item.title: Who Is Ahead in Alaska's At-Large Congressional District?
item.description: Track the latest polls in Alaska's At-Large Congressional District.

item.title: Militares de EE. UU. y de Cuba se reúnen fuera de la base de Guantánamo
item.description: La reunión fue el encuentro militar de más alto nivel desde que el gobierno de Donald Trump comenzó a aumentar la presión sobre el gobierno cubano este año.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg, Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Comando Sur de Estados Unidos/Agence France-Presse - Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Una foto compartida en las redes sociales por el Comando Sur de Estados Unidos, que muestra al general Francis L. Donovan (centro) con oficiales militares cubanos, durante una reunión en el perímetro de la Estación Naval de Estados Unidos en la bahía de Guantánamo, Cuba, el viernes.

item.title: U.S. and Cuban Military Officials Meet Outside of Guantánamo Base
item.description: The meeting was the highest-level military engagement since the Trump administration began ratcheting up pressure on the Cuban government this year.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg, Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: U.S. Southern Command/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A photo shared on social media by U.S. Southern Command, showing Gen. Francis L. Donovan (center) with Cuban military officials, during a meeting at the perimeter of the U.S. Naval Station in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, on Friday.

item.title: Iran’s Team Trains in Limbo for World Cup Overshadowed by War
item.description: The Iranian soccer players, still awaiting visas to the U.S., are practicing in Turkey and making backup plans.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Umit Bektas/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Members of Iran’s national soccer team practicing in Antalya, Turkey, on Thursday.

item.description: School children chanting praise to Shivaji, the Maratha king, by his statue in Malvan, Maharashtra, in January.

item.title: A Frosty Glass of Greens
item.description: Ali Slagle’s green smoothie is my favorite low-effort way to get my greens.
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Liebe for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne. Prop Stylist: Megan Hedgpeth.
media:content.media:description: Veggies Natural Juice Bar & Cafe’s green smoothie, adapted by Ali Slagle.

item.title: A Big Bang, a Fire and Panic as War Enters Romanian Homes
item.description: When a drone crashed into an apartment building in eastern Romania, residents were reminded that the Russia-Ukraine war makes for a dangerous neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Andreea Campeanu and Andrew Higgins

media:content.media:credit: Andreea Campeanu for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A temporary cover for a hole in the roof of a building in Galati, Romania, on Saturday, after a Russian drone slammed into a residential block on Friday.

item.title: Living With Cancer: Personal Stories
item.description: Readers react to a guest essay about how some patients are living longer with advanced cancer.

item.title: How to Make Cosmic Blondies
item.description: These easy bars are crowned with swooshes of colorful white chocolate ganache and showers of glitter.
item.dc:creator: Vaughn Vreeland

media:content.media:credit: Julia Gartland for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Rebecca Jurkevich.
media:content.media:description: Vaughn Vreeland’s cosmic blondies.

item.title: Cómo lograr que tus hijos se acerquen más a sus abuelos
item.description: Intercambiar mensajes de texto o audios tiene beneficios para ambas generaciones. Los expertos comparten ideas.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Passarella

item.title: What I Saw Inside an Ebola Ward
item.description: Our chief Africa correspondent, Declan Walsh, walked inside an Ebola ward where both adults and children were being treated, and where someone had recently died of the disease amid a spiraling outbreak in the region. This is what he saw.
item.dc:creator: Declan Walsh, Estelle Caswell, Thomas Vollkommer and Arlette Bashizi

item.title: As Trump Mulls Decision About Iran War Deal, a Restive Middle East Waits to Hear
item.description: The president has wavered on whether to move ahead with an agreement with Iran to end the war. On Friday, he vowed to make a “final determination” soon.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Leily Nikounazar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump said on Friday that he was meeting with advisers to make a “final determination” about a proposed deal with Iran.

item.title: Ye and Travis Scott Concerts Canceled in Italy Over Security Fears
item.description: The events were canceled “to ensure public order and safety,” the authorities said. Jewish leaders had objected to the concert by Ye, formerly known as Kanye West, who has a history of antisemitic remarks.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg

item.title: When War Breaks Out Over the Family Business, They Get the Call
item.description: As baby boomers begin passing down trillions in assets, advisers who specialize in navigating the process — and the emotions around it — have never been busier.
item.dc:creator: Brent Crane

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Doug Baumoel founded Continuity Family Business Consulting, in Massachusetts, to “help families prevent, manage and heal from conflict over business and money.”
